# File: `docs/acceptance-criteria.md`

```md
# Acceptance Criteria — e-commerce-web MVP

## 1. General Acceptance Criteria

### AC-GEN-001 — Project Documentation Scope

Given Wave 1 Product Documentation is complete  
When PM reviews the documentation  
Then `docs/prd.md` and `docs/acceptance-criteria.md` must exist  
And both files must describe Customer, Admin, Auth, Product, Category and Order scope  
And acceptance criteria must be testable by QA.

---

### AC-GEN-002 — Supported Roles

Given the MVP is implemented  
When users interact with the system  
Then the system must support exactly these roles for MVP:

- Guest
- Customer
- Admin

And role-based access must be enforced for protected features.

---

### AC-GEN-003 — Password Exposure

Given any API returns user information  
When the response body is inspected  
Then `password`, `passwordHash`, or any equivalent password secret must not be included.

---

### AC-GEN-004 — Error Handling

Given a request fails due to validation, authentication, authorization or missing resource  
When the API returns an error  
Then the response must include:

- HTTP status code
- Machine-readable error code or type
- Human-readable message

And the frontend must display a user-understandable message for form validation errors.

---

## 2. Authentication Acceptance Criteria

## 2.1 Customer Registration

### AC-AUTH-001 — Register Customer Successfully

Given a guest is on the registration page  
When they submit:

- A non-empty name
- A valid unique email
- A password meeting backend validation rules

Then the system must create a new user with role `CUSTOMER`  
And the password must be stored as a bcrypt hash  
And the response must not include password hash  
And the user must be able to log in with that email and password.

---

### AC-AUTH-002 — Reject Duplicate Email

Given a user already exists with email `test@example.com`  
When a guest attempts to register using `test@example.com`  
Then the system must reject the registration  
And return/display a clear duplicate email error  
And no duplicate user record must be created.

---

### AC-AUTH-003 — Reject Invalid Registration Input

Given a guest submits the registration form  
When name is empty, email is invalid, or password is missing/invalid  
Then the system must reject the registration  
And show field-specific validation errors  
And no user record must be created.

---

## 2.2 Login

### AC-AUTH-004 — Customer Login Successfully

Given a customer account exists  
When the customer submits correct email and password  
Then the system must authenticate the user  
And return/store a JWT according to frontend/backend design  
And identify the user as role `CUSTOMER`.

---

### AC-AUTH-005 — Reject Incorrect Login

Given a user account exists  
When the login password is incorrect  
Then the system must reject login  
And must not issue a JWT  
And must show a clear invalid credentials message.

---

### AC-AUTH-006 — Admin Login Successfully

Given an admin account exists  
When the admin submits correct email and password on admin login  
Then the system must authenticate the admin  
And identify the user as role `ADMIN`  
And allow access to admin dashboard.

---

### AC-AUTH-007 — Customer Cannot Access Admin

Given a logged-in customer has role `CUSTOMER`  
When they attempt to access any admin route or admin API  
Then the system must deny access  
And return or display an unauthorized/forbidden error  
And must not expose admin data.

---

### AC-AUTH-008 — Guest Cannot Access Protected Customer Features

Given a guest is not logged in  
When they attempt to access checkout, order history, or order detail  
Then the system must require login  
And must not create or reveal order data.

---

## 3. Customer Site Acceptance Criteria

## 3.1 Home Page

### AC-HOME-001 — Home Page Loads

Given a guest or customer opens the home page  
When the page loads successfully  
Then the page must display:

- Store/site name
- Navigation to product list
- Navigation to cart
- Navigation to login/account area
- At least one product section if seed products exist

---

## 3.2 Product List

### AC-PROD-LIST-001 — Display Active Products

Given active products exist in the database  
When a guest/customer opens the product listing page  
Then the page must display active products only  
And each product card must show:

- Product name
- Price
- Image or placeholder
- Stock availability indicator
- Link/action to view detail

---

### AC-PROD-LIST-002 — Do Not Display Inactive Products Publicly

Given a product has `isActive = false`  
When a guest/customer opens the product listing page  
Then that inactive product must not appear in the public list.

---

### AC-PROD-LIST-003 — Empty Product List State

Given there are no active products  
When a guest/customer opens the product listing page  
Then the page must show an empty state message  
And must not crash.

---

## 3.3 Product Detail

### AC-PROD-DETAIL-001 — Display Product Detail

Given an active product exists  
When a guest/customer opens its product detail page  
Then the page must display:

- Product name
- Description
- Price
- Category
- Stock quantity or stock availability
- Image or placeholder

---

### AC-PROD-DETAIL-002 — Reject Missing Product Detail

Given a product id does not exist or product is inactive  
When a guest/customer opens that product detail page  
Then the system must show a not found or unavailable message  
And must not show invalid product data.

---

### AC-PROD-DETAIL-003 — Add In-Stock Product to Cart

Given an active product has stock greater than 0  
When a guest/customer clicks add to cart with quantity 1  
Then the product must be added to cart  
And the cart item quantity must be 1  
And cart total must update accordingly.

---

### AC-PROD-DETAIL-004 — Cannot Add Out-of-Stock Product to Cart

Given an active product has stock equal to 0  
When a guest/customer attempts to add it to cart  
Then the system must prevent the add action  
And show an out-of-stock message.

---

## 3.4 Cart

### AC-CART-001 — View Cart Items

Given a cart contains one or more items  
When a guest/customer opens the cart page  
Then the page must display each item with:

- Product name
- Unit price
- Quantity
- Line total

And the page must display cart total.

---

### AC-CART-002 — Update Cart Quantity

Given a cart item exists  
When the user changes the quantity to a valid integer greater than or equal to 1  
Then the cart item quantity must update  
And line total and cart total must be recalculated.

---

### AC-CART-003 — Remove Cart Item

Given a cart item exists  
When the user removes the item  
Then the item must no longer appear in the cart  
And cart total must be recalculated.

---

### AC-CART-004 — Empty Cart State

Given the cart has no items  
When the user opens the cart page  
Then the page must show an empty cart message  
And checkout action must be unavailable or redirect appropriately.

---

### AC-CART-005 — Cart Must Not Trust Client Price

Given a user has items in cart  
When checkout is submitted  
Then backend must calculate order prices using current database product prices  
And must not trust unit price sent from client.

---

## 3.5 Checkout COD

### AC-CHECKOUT-001 — Require Login for Checkout

Given a guest has items in cart  
When they attempt to checkout  
Then the system must require login  
And must not create an order until the user is authenticated as customer.

---

### AC-CHECKOUT-002 — Checkout Successfully

Given a logged-in customer has a cart with valid active products  
And requested quantities do not exceed stock  
When the customer submits checkout with:

- Shipping name
- Shipping phone
- Shipping address
- Optional note

Then the system must create an order with status `PENDING`  
And payment method `COD`  
And create order items with product snapshot data  
And calculate subtotal/total on backend  
And decrease product stock by ordered quantity  
And clear the cart after success  
And show order confirmation or order detail.

---

### AC-CHECKOUT-003 — Reject Checkout With Empty Cart

Given a logged-in customer has an empty cart  
When they attempt to submit checkout  
Then the system must reject checkout  
And no order must be created.

---

### AC-CHECKOUT-004 — Reject Checkout With Insufficient Stock

Given a product has stock 2  
And a customer cart requests quantity 3 for that product  
When the customer submits checkout  
Then the system must reject checkout  
And show an insufficient stock message  
And no order must be created  
And stock must remain unchanged.

---

### AC-CHECKOUT-005 — Reject Checkout With Inactive Product

Given a cart contains a product that is no longer active  
When the customer submits checkout  
Then the system must reject checkout  
And identify that one or more products are unavailable  
And no order must be created.

---

### AC-CHECKOUT-006 — Required Shipping Fields

Given a logged-in customer is on checkout page  
When they submit checkout with missing shipping name, phone or address  
Then the system must reject checkout  
And show field-specific validation errors  
And no order must be created.

---

## 3.6 Customer Order History

### AC-ORDER-HIST-001 — Customer Views Own Orders

Given a logged-in customer has existing orders  
When they open order history  
Then the system must display only orders belonging to that customer  
And each order row/card must show:

- Order number or id
- Created date
- Total
- Status
- Payment method

---

### AC-ORDER-HIST-002 — Customer With No Orders

Given a logged-in customer has no orders  
When they open order history  
Then the page must show a no orders message  
And must not crash.

---

### AC-ORDER-HIST-003 — Customer Cannot View Other Customer Orders

Given Customer A and Customer B exist  
And Customer A is logged in  
When Customer A attempts to access Customer B's order detail by id  
Then the system must deny access or return not found  
And must not expose Customer B's order data.

---

## 3.7 Customer Order Detail

### AC-ORDER-DETAIL-001 — View Own Order Detail

Given a logged-in customer has an order  
When they open that order detail  
Then the page must display:

- Order number or id
- Order status
- Payment method
- Shipping name
- Shipping phone
- Shipping address
- Ordered items
- Unit price snapshot
- Quantity
- Line total
- Order total
- Created date

---

## 4. Admin Site Acceptance Criteria

## 4.1 Admin Dashboard

### AC-ADMIN-DASH-001 — Dashboard Loads For Admin

Given an admin is logged in  
When they open admin dashboard  
Then the dashboard must display:

- Total product count
- Total category count
- Total order count
- Total customer count
- Revenue from non-cancelled completed or valid orders, according to technical spec

---

### AC-ADMIN-DASH-002 — Dashboard Denies Non-Admin

Given a guest or customer attempts to access dashboard  
When the page/API is requested  
Then access must be denied  
And dashboard metrics must not be exposed.

---

## 4.2 Category Management

### AC-ADMIN-CAT-001 — Admin Lists Categories

Given an admin is logged in  
When they open category management  
Then the system must display categories with:

- Name
- Slug or identifier
- Active status
- Created/updated timestamp if available

---

### AC-ADMIN-CAT-002 — Admin Creates Category

Given an admin is logged in  
When they submit a valid category name  
Then the system must create the category  
And the new category must appear in admin category list.

---

### AC-ADMIN-CAT-003 — Reject Invalid Category

Given an admin is logged in  
When they submit an empty category name  
Then the system must reject the request  
And show a validation error  
And no category must be created.

---

### AC-ADMIN-CAT-004 — Admin Updates Category

Given a category exists  
And an admin is logged in  
When the admin updates category name or active status  
Then the system must save the changes  
And updated data must appear after refresh.

---

### AC-ADMIN-CAT-005 — Inactive Category Hidden Publicly

Given a category is set inactive  
When a guest/customer opens public category list  
Then the inactive category must not appear.

---

## 4.3 Product Management

### AC-ADMIN-PROD-001 — Admin Lists Products

Given an admin is logged in  
When they open product management  
Then the system must display products with:

- Name
- Category
- Price
- Stock
- Active status

---

### AC-ADMIN-PROD-002 — Admin Creates Product

Given an admin is logged in  
And at least one category exists  
When they submit a product with:

- Name
- Valid category
- Price greater than or equal to 0
- Stock greater than or equal to 0
- Optional description
- Optional image URL
- Active status

Then the system must create the product  
And the product must appear in admin product list.

---

### AC-ADMIN-PROD-003 — Reject Invalid Product

Given an admin is logged in  
When they submit a product with empty name, invalid category, negative price, or negative stock  
Then the system must reject the request  
And show validation errors  
And no product must be created or updated.

---

### AC-ADMIN-PROD-004 — Admin Updates Product

Given a product exists  
And an admin is logged in  
When they update product name, description, category, price, stock, image URL or active status  
Then the system must save the changes  
And updated data must appear after refresh.

---

### AC-ADMIN-PROD-005 — Inactive Product Hidden Publicly

Given an admin sets a product inactive  
When a guest/customer opens public product list  
Then that product must not appear.

---

### AC-ADMIN-PROD-006 — Product Stock Affects Checkout

Given an admin updates product stock to 0  
When a customer attempts to checkout that product  
Then checkout must be rejected due to insufficient stock.

---

## 4.4 Order Management

### AC-ADMIN-ORDER-001 — Admin Lists Orders

Given an admin is logged in  
When they open order management  
Then the system must display all orders with:

- Order number or id
- Customer name or email
- Created date
- Total
- Status
- Payment method

---

### AC-ADMIN-ORDER-002 — Admin Views Order Detail

Given an order exists  
And an admin is logged in  
When they open order detail  
Then the system must display:

- Customer information
- Shipping information
- Ordered items
- Unit price snapshot
- Quantity
- Line total
- Total
- Status
- Payment method
- Created date

---

### AC-ADMIN-ORDER-003 — Admin Updates Valid Order Status

Given an order has status `PENDING`  
And an admin is logged in  
When the admin updates status to `CONFIRMED`  
Then the system must save the new status  
And the updated status must be visible to admin and customer.

---

### AC-ADMIN-ORDER-004 — Reject Invalid Order Status Transition

Given an order has status `COMPLETED`  
And an admin is logged in  
When the admin attempts to update it to `PENDING`, `CONFIRMED`, `SHIPPING`, or `CANCELLED`  
Then the system must reject the update  
And keep status as `COMPLETED`.

---

### AC-ADMIN-ORDER-005 — Cancel Order Restores Stock

Given an order has status `PENDING` or `CONFIRMED`  
And the order contains product quantity 2  
When an admin changes the order status to `CANCELLED`  
Then the product stock must increase by 2  
And the order status must become `CANCELLED`.

---

### AC-ADMIN-ORDER-006 — Cancelled Order Cannot Be Updated

Given an order has status `CANCELLED`  
When an admin attempts to update it to another status  
Then the system must reject the update  
And keep status as `CANCELLED`.

---

## 4.5 Customer Management

### AC-ADMIN-CUST-001 — Admin Lists Customers

Given an admin is logged in  
When they open customer management  
Then the system must display users with role `CUSTOMER`  
And each row/card must include:

- Name
- Email
- Active status if supported
- Created date if available

And must not include password hash.

---

### AC-ADMIN-CUST-002 — Admin Views Customer Detail

Given a customer exists  
And an admin is logged in  
When the admin opens customer detail  
Then the system must display:

- Customer name
- Customer email
- Customer active status if supported
- Customer order list

And must not display password hash.

---

### AC-ADMIN-CUST-003 — Customer Cannot Access Customer Management

Given a logged-in customer attempts to access customer management API or page  
Then access must be denied  
And customer list must not be exposed.

---

## 5. API and Authorization Acceptance Criteria

### AC-API-001 — Public Product APIs

Given a guest calls public product list/detail APIs  
When products are active  
Then API must return active products only  
And must not require JWT.

---

### AC-API-002 — Protected Customer Order APIs

Given a request to customer order APIs has no JWT  
When the request is processed  
Then API must return unauthorized  
And no order data must be returned.

---

### AC-API-003 — Admin APIs Require Admin Role

Given a request to admin APIs has a valid customer JWT  
When the request is processed  
Then API must return forbidden  
And no admin data must be returned.

---

### AC-API-004 — Invalid JWT Rejected

Given a request contains an invalid or expired JWT  
When accessing protected APIs  
Then API must return unauthorized  
And must not process the protected action.

---

## 6. Database and Data Integrity Acceptance Criteria

### AC-DB-001 — Seed Data Exists

Given the local database has been seeded  
When the app starts in local MVP mode  
Then there must be at minimum:

- 1 admin user
- 1 customer user
- 3 categories
- 8 active products

---

### AC-DB-002 — Order Item Snapshot

Given a customer creates an order  
When the order item is saved  
Then each order item must store:

- Product id
- Product name at time of order
- Unit price at time of order
- Quantity
- Line total

---

### AC-DB-003 — Product Price Change Does Not Change Past Order

Given an order exists with product unit price 100  
When admin later changes product price to 150  
Then the existing order item must still show unit price 100.

---

### AC-DB-004 — Stock Decreases On Order Creation

Given product stock is 10  
When customer successfully orders quantity 3  
Then product stock must become 7.

---

### AC-DB-005 — Failed Checkout Does Not Change Stock

Given product stock is 10  
When checkout fails due to validation, authentication, inactive product or insufficient stock  
Then product stock must remain 10  
And no order must be created.

---

## 7. Frontend UX Acceptance Criteria

### AC-UX-001 — Navigation Available

Given any user is on customer site  
When page is loaded  
Then navigation must provide access to:

- Home
- Products
- Cart
- Login/account area

---

### AC-UX-002 — Admin Navigation Available

Given admin is logged in  
When admin site loads  
Then navigation must provide access to:

- Dashboard
- Categories
- Products
- Orders
- Customers
- Logout

---

### AC-UX-003 — Form Validation Display

Given a user submits an invalid form  
When validation fails  
Then the UI must display clear error messages near or associated with invalid fields.

---

### AC-UX-004 — Loading and Error States

Given frontend is requesting API data  
When the request is pending  
Then a loading indicator or loading text must be shown  
And when the request fails  
Then an error message must be shown  
And the page must not crash.

---

## 8. MVP Completion Criteria

The MVP is accepted when all of the following are true:

- Guest can browse product list and detail.
- Customer can register and login.
- Customer can add products to cart.
- Customer can checkout COD successfully.
- Customer can view own order history and detail.
- Customer cannot view another customer's order.
- Admin can login.
- Admin can manage categories.
- Admin can manage products.
- Admin can manage orders and valid status transitions.
- Admin can view customers.
- Customer cannot access admin features.
- Guest cannot access protected customer/admin features.
- Stock is updated correctly after checkout and cancellation.
- API never exposes password hash.
- Seed data exists for local demo.
```

architecture-agent.text:
# Technical Architecture & API Contract — Website Bán Hàng MVP

> Phiên bản: `v1.0-draft`  
> Phạm vi: MVP ecommerce cơ bản theo PRD  
> API Base URL đề xuất: `/api/v1`  
> Auth: JWT Bearer Token  
> Payment MVP: COD  
> Vai trò: `customer`, `admin`

---

## 1. Kiến trúc tổng quan

## 1.1. Mô hình hệ thống

```txt
Frontend Web
  ├── Customer Website
  └── Admin Dashboard
        │
        ▼
Backend REST API
  ├── Auth Module
  ├── Product Module
  ├── Category Module
  ├── Cart Module
  ├── Order Module
  ├── User/Admin Module
  └── Dashboard Module
        │
        ▼
Database
  ├── users
  ├── categories
  ├── products
  ├── product_images
  ├── carts
  ├── cart_items
  ├── orders
  └── order_items
```

---

## 1.2. Công nghệ đề xuất

Không bắt buộc, nhưng phù hợp MVP:

### Frontend

- React / Next.js
- TypeScript
- Tailwind CSS hoặc Ant Design cho admin
- State management: Zustand / Redux Toolkit
- Form validation: React Hook Form + Zod

### Backend

- Node.js NestJS / Express hoặc Laravel / Spring Boot
- REST API
- JWT Authentication
- Role-based Authorization

### Database

- PostgreSQL hoặc MySQL
- Redis optional cho cache/session sau MVP

### File Storage

MVP có thể dùng:

- Local upload folder
- Hoặc S3-compatible storage nếu cần production

---

# 2. Quy ước API chung

## 2.1. Base URL

```txt
/api/v1
```

---

## 2.2. Authentication Header

Các API cần đăng nhập sử dụng:

```http
Authorization: Bearer <access_token>
```

---

## 2.3. Format Response thành công

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Success",
  "data": {}
}
```

Với danh sách phân trang:

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Success",
  "data": {
    "items": [],
    "pagination": {
      "page": 1,
      "limit": 20,
      "total": 100,
      "total_pages": 5
    }
  }
}
```

---

## 2.4. Format Response lỗi

```json
{
  "success": false,
  "message": "Validation error",
  "errors": [
    {
      "field": "email",
      "message": "Email không hợp lệ"
    }
  ]
}
```

---

## 2.5. HTTP Status Code

| Code | Ý nghĩa |
|---|---|
| `200` | Thành công |
| `201` | Tạo mới thành công |
| `400` | Request không hợp lệ |
| `401` | Chưa đăng nhập |
| `403` | Không có quyền |
| `404` | Không tìm thấy |
| `409` | Xung đột dữ liệu, ví dụ email/slug đã tồn tại |
| `422` | Validation error |
| `500` | Lỗi server |

---

# 3. Data Model

## 3.1. users

```sql
users {
  id: uuid PK
  full_name: varchar(255)
  email: varchar(255) unique
  phone: varchar(20)
  password_hash: varchar(255)
  role: enum('customer', 'admin')
  status: enum('active', 'locked')
  created_at: timestamp
  updated_at: timestamp
}
```

---

## 3.2. categories

```sql
categories {
  id: uuid PK
  name: varchar(255)
  slug: varchar(255) unique
  description: text nullable
  is_active: boolean default true
  created_at: timestamp
  updated_at: timestamp
}
```

---

## 3.3. products

```sql
products {
  id: uuid PK
  name: varchar(255)
  slug: varchar(255) unique
  category_id: uuid FK -> categories.id
  thumbnail: varchar(500)
  original_price: decimal(12,2)
  sale_price: decimal(12,2) nullable
  short_description: text nullable
  description: text nullable
  stock_quantity: int default 0
  is_active: boolean default true
  is_featured: boolean default false
  created_at: timestamp
  updated_at: timestamp
}
```

> PRD đang để `category_id`, nghĩa là mỗi sản phẩm thuộc một danh mục. Nếu cần nhiều danh mục/sản phẩm, nên đổi sang bảng trung gian `product_categories`.

---

## 3.4. product_images

```sql
product_images {
  id: uuid PK
  product_id: uuid FK -> products.id
  image_url: varchar(500)
  sort_order: int default 0
  created_at: timestamp
}
```

---

## 3.5. carts

```sql
carts {
  id: uuid PK
  user_id: uuid nullable FK -> users.id
  session_id: varchar(255) nullable
  created_at: timestamp
  updated_at: timestamp
}
```

---

## 3.6. cart_items

```sql
cart_items {
  id: uuid PK
  cart_id: uuid FK -> carts.id
  product_id: uuid FK -> products.id
  quantity: int
  created_at: timestamp
  updated_at: timestamp
}
```

Unique constraint:

```txt
unique(cart_id, product_id)
```

---

## 3.7. orders

```sql
orders {
  id: uuid PK
  order_code: varchar(50) unique
  user_id: uuid FK -> users.id
  receiver_name: varchar(255)
  receiver_phone: varchar(20)
  receiver_address: text
  note: text nullable
  payment_method: enum('COD')
  total_amount: decimal(12,2)
  status: enum(
    'pending',
    'confirmed',
    'shipping',
    'completed',
    'cancelled'
  )
  created_at: timestamp
  updated_at: timestamp
}
```

Mapping hiển thị:

| DB status | Tiếng Việt |
|---|---|
| `pending` | Chờ xác nhận |
| `confirmed` | Đã xác nhận |
| `shipping` | Đang giao hàng |
| `completed` | Hoàn tất |
| `cancelled` | Đã hủy |

---

## 3.8. order_items

```sql
order_items {
  id: uuid PK
  order_id: uuid FK -> orders.id
  product_id: uuid nullable FK -> products.id
  product_name: varchar(255)
  product_thumbnail: varchar(500) nullable
  unit_price: decimal(12,2)
  quantity: int
  subtotal: decimal(12,2)
}
```

---

# 5. Public APIs — Customer Website

---

# 5.1. Auth APIs

## 5.1.1. Đăng ký

```http
POST /api/v1/auth/register
```

### Request

```json
{
  "full_name": "Nguyễn Văn A",
  "email": "customer@example.com",
  "phone": "0901234567",
  "password": "password123",
  "password_confirmation": "password123"
}
```

### Validation

| Field | Rule |
|---|---|
| `full_name` | required |
| `email` | required, email, unique |
| `phone` | required, 10-11 digits |
| `password` | required, min 8 |
| `password_confirmation` | required, same password |

### Response `201`

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Đăng ký thành công",
  "data": {
    "user": {
      "id": "uuid",
      "full_name": "Nguyễn Văn A",
      "email": "customer@example.com",
      "phone": "0901234567",
      "role": "customer",
      "status": "active"
    }
  }
}
```

### Error

- `409`: Email đã tồn tại
- `422`: Validation error

---

## 5.1.2. Đăng nhập

```http
POST /api/v1/auth/login
```

### Request

```json
{
  "email": "customer@example.com",
  "password": "password123"
}
```

### Response `200`

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Đăng nhập thành công",
  "data": {
    "access_token": "jwt_token",
    "token_type": "Bearer",
    "expires_in": 86400,
    "user": {
      "id": "uuid",
      "full_name": "Nguyễn Văn A",
      "email": "customer@example.com",
      "phone": "0901234567",
      "role": "customer",
      "status": "active"
    }
  }
}
```

### Error

- `401`: Email hoặc mật khẩu không chính xác
- `403`: Tài khoản đã bị khóa

---

## 5.1.3. Lấy thông tin user hiện tại

```http
GET /api/v1/auth/me
```

Auth required.

### Response `200`

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Success",
  "data": {
    "id": "uuid",
    "full_name": "Nguyễn Văn A",
    "email": "customer@example.com",
    "phone": "0901234567",
    "role": "customer",
    "status": "active"
  }
}

```

Auth required.

### Response `200`

# 5.3. Product APIs

## 5.3.1. Danh sách sản phẩm

```http
GET /api/v1/products
```

### Query Params

| Param | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| `page` | number | no | Default `1` |
| `limit` | number | no | Default `20`, max `20` |
| `q` | string | no | Tìm theo tên hoặc mô tả ngắn |
| `category_id` | uuid | no | Lọc theo danh mục |
| `category_slug` | string | no | Lọc theo slug danh mục |
| `min_price` | number | no | Giá thấp nhất |
| `max_price` | number | no | Giá cao nhất |
| `in_stock` | boolean | no | `true` lọc còn hàng |
| `sort` | string | no | `newest`, `price_asc`, `price_desc` |
| `is_featured` | boolean | no | Lọc sản phẩm nổi bật |

### Business Rules

- Chỉ trả về sản phẩm `is_active = true`.
- Chỉ trả về danh mục `is_active = true`.
- Giá dùng để filter/sort là `sale_price` nếu có, ngược lại dùng `original_price`.

### Response `200`

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Success",
  "data": {
    "items": [
      {
        "id": "uuid",
        "name": "Áo thun nam",
        "slug": "ao-thun-nam",
        "thumbnail": "https://example.com/image.jpg",
        "original_price": 200000,
        "sale_price": 150000,
        "display_price": 150000,
        "stock_quantity": 10,
        "is_in_stock": true,
        "is_featured": true,
        "category": {
          "id": "uuid",
          "name": "Thời trang",
          "slug": "thoi-trang"
        }
      }
    ],
    "pagination": {
      "page": 1,
      "limit": 20,
      "total": 1,
      "total_pages": 1
    }
  }
}
```

---

# 5.4. Cart APIs

> MVP hỗ trợ cart cho khách vãng lai bằng `session_id` và cart cho user đăng nhập bằng `user_id`.

## Header cho guest cart

Frontend gửi header:

```http
X-Session-Id: <uuid>
```

Nếu chưa có, frontend tự tạo UUID và lưu localStorage.

---

## 5.4.1. Lấy giỏ hàng

```http
GET /api/v1/cart
```

Auth optional. Nếu đăng nhập dùng `user_id`, nếu chưa đăng nhập dùng `X-Session-Id`.

### Response `200`

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Success",
  "data": {
    "id": "uuid",
    "items": [
      {
        "id": "uuid",
        "product": {
          "id": "uuid",
          "name": "Áo thun nam",
          "slug": "ao-thun-nam",
          "thumbnail": "https://example.com/image.jpg",
          "original_price": 200000,
          "sale_price": 150000,
          "display_price": 150000,
          "stock_quantity": 10
        },
        "quantity": 2,
        "unit_price": 150000,
        "subtotal": 300000
      }
    ],
    "total_quantity": 2,
    "total_amount": 300000
  }
}
```

---

## 5.4.2. Thêm sản phẩm vào giỏ

```http
POST /api/v1/cart/items
```

Auth optional.

### Request

```json
{
  "product_id": "uuid",
  "quantity": 1
}
```

### Validation

| Field | Rule |
|---|---|
| `product_id` | required, exists |
| `quantity` | required, integer, min 1 |

### Response `200`

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Đã thêm sản phẩm vào giỏ hàng",
  "data": {
    "cart_id": "uuid",
    "total_quantity": 3,
    "total_amount": 450000
  }
}
```

### Error

- `404`: Sản phẩm không tồn tại
- `400`: Sản phẩm đã hết hàng
- `422`: Số lượng vượt quá tồn kho

---

```http
PATCH /api/v1/cart/items/{item_id}
```

Auth optional.

### Request

  "quantity": 3
}
```

### Response `200`

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Cập nhật giỏ hàng thành công",
  "data": {
    "item_id": "uuid",
    "quantity": 3,
    "subtotal": 450000,
    "total_amount": 450000
  }
}
```

### Error

- `404`: Sản phẩm trong giỏ không tồn tại
- `422`: Số lượng không hợp lệ hoặc vượt quá tồn kho

# 5.5. Checkout / Order APIs

## 5.5.1. Tạo đơn hàng

```http
POST /api/v1/orders
```

Auth required: customer.

### Request

```json
{
  "receiver_name": "Nguyễn Văn A",
  "receiver_phone": "0901234567",
  "receiver_address": "123 Nguyễn Trãi, Quận 1, TP.HCM",
  "note": "Giao giờ hành chính",
  "payment_method": "COD"
}
```

### Validation

| Field | Rule |
|---|---|
| `receiver_name` | required |
| `receiver_phone` | required, 10-11 digits |
| `receiver_address` | required |
| `payment_method` | required, enum `COD` |

### Business Rules

- User phải đăng nhập.
- Cart phải có ít nhất 1 item.
- Kiểm tra sản phẩm còn active.
- Kiểm tra tồn kho từng sản phẩm.
- Khi tạo order thành công:
  - Tạo `orders`.
  - Tạo `order_items`.
  - Trừ tồn kho.
  - Xóa cart.
  - Status mặc định: `pending`.

### Response `201`

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Đặt hàng thành công",
  "data": {
    "order_id": "uuid",
    "order_code": "ORD202601010001",
    "status": "pending",
    "status_label": "Chờ xác nhận",
    "total_amount": 300000
  }
}
```

### Error

- `401`: Chưa đăng nhập
- `400`: Giỏ hàng của bạn đang trống
- `422`: Sản phẩm không đủ tồn kho

Example error:

```json
{
  "success": false,
  "message": "Một số sản phẩm không đủ tồn kho",
  "errors": [
    {
      "field": "items",
      "message": "Sản phẩm Áo thun nam chỉ còn 1 sản phẩm"
    }
  ]
}
```

---

## 5.5.2. Lịch sử đơn hàng của tôi

```http
GET /api/v1/orders/my
```

Auth required: customer.

### Query Params

| Param | Type | Required |
|---|---:|---:|
| `page` | number | no |
| `limit` | number | no |
| `status` | string | no |

### Response `200`

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Success",
  "data": {
    "items": [
      {
        "id": "uuid",
        "order_code": "ORD202601010001",
        "total_amount": 300000,
        "status": "pending",
        "status_label": "Chờ xác nhận",
        "created_at": "2026-01-01T00:00:00Z"
      }
    ],
    "pagination": {
      "page": 1,
      "limit": 20,
      "total": 1,
      "total_pages": 1
    }
  }
}
```

---

## 5.5.3. Chi tiết đơn hàng của tôi

```http
GET /api/v1/orders/my/{order_id}
```

Auth required: customer.

### Response `200`

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Success",
  "data": {
    "id": "uuid",
    "order_code": "ORD202601010001",
    "receiver_name": "Nguyễn Văn A",
    "receiver_phone": "0901234567",
    "receiver_address": "123 Nguyễn Trãi, Quận 1, TP.HCM",
    "note": "Giao giờ hành chính",
    "payment_method": "COD",
    "total_amount": 300000,
    "status": "pending",
    "status_label": "Chờ xác nhận",
    "items": [
      {
        "id": "uuid",
        "product_id": "uuid",
        "product_name": "Áo thun nam",
        "product_thumbnail": "https://example.com/image.jpg",
        "unit_price": 150000,
        "quantity": 2,
        "subtotal": 300000
      }
    ],
    "created_at": "2026-01-01T00:00:00Z"
  }
}
```

### Error

> Backend phải đảm bảo user chỉ xem được order của chính mình.

# 6.2. Admin Category APIs

GET /api/v1/admin/categories
```

### Query Params

| Param | Type | Required |
|---|---:|---:|

## 6.4.3. Cập nhật trạng thái đơn hàng

```http
PATCH /api/v1/admin/orders/{id}/status
```

### Request

```json
{
  "status": "confirmed"
}
```

### Status Flow hợp lệ

```txt
pending    -> confirmed
confirmed  -> shipping
shipping   -> completed
pending    -> cancelled
confirmed  -> cancelled
```

Không hợp lệ:

```txt
completed -> any
cancelled -> any
shipping -> cancelled, pending, confirmed
```

### Response `200`

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Cập nhật trạng thái đơn hàng thành công",
  "data": {
    "id": "uuid",
    "order_code": "ORD202601010001",
    "status": "confirmed",
    "status_label": "Đã xác nhận"
  }
}
```

### Error

- `400`: Không thể chuyển trạng thái đơn hàng theo luồng này
- `404`: Đơn hàng không tồn tại

### Business Rules

- Khi chuyển sang `cancelled`, hoàn lại tồn kho cho từng order item.
- Không hoàn kho nhiều lần nếu order đã `cancelled`.
- Khi order `completed`, không cho sửa trạng thái nữa.

# 8. Business Rules quan trọng

## 8.1. Tính giá sản phẩm

```txt
display_price = sale_price nếu sale_price != null
display_price = original_price nếu sale_price == null
```

Điều kiện hợp lệ:

```txt
0 <= sale_price <= original_price
```

---

## 8.2. Tồn kho

Khi thêm vào giỏ:

```txt
cart_item.quantity <= product.stock_quantity
```

Khi tạo đơn hàng:

```txt
order_item.quantity <= product.stock_quantity
```

Sau khi tạo đơn hàng thành công:

```txt
product.stock_quantity -= order_item.quantity
```

Khi đơn hàng bị hủy:

```txt
product.stock_quantity += order_item.quantity
```

---

## 8.3. Tính tổng tiền đơn hàng

```txt
unit_price = product.sale_price nếu có, ngược lại product.original_price
subtotal = unit_price * quantity
total_amount = sum(order_items.subtotal)
```

Order phải snapshot thông tin sản phẩm tại thời điểm mua:

- `product_name`
- `product_thumbnail`
- `unit_price`

---

## 8.4. Quyền truy cập

| API group | Guest | Customer | Admin |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| Public products/categories | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Cart | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Checkout/order create | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ technically, but should use customer flow |
| My orders | ❌ | ✅ | ❌/optional |
| Admin APIs | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ |

# 10. Index Database đề xuất

```sql
users:
  unique(email)
  index(role)
  index(status)

categories:
  unique(slug)
  index(is_active)

products:
  unique(slug)
  index(category_id)
  index(is_active)
  index(is_featured)
  index(stock_quantity)
  index(created_at)

orders:
  unique(order_code)
  index(user_id)
  index(status)
  index(created_at)
  index(receiver_phone)

order_items:
  index(order_id)
  index(product_id)

cart_items:
  unique(cart_id, product_id)
```

# 11. Open Questions cần PM/Product xác nhận

Các điểm cần xác nhận trước khi lock API contract:

1. Sản phẩm thuộc **một danh mục** hay **nhiều danh mục**?
   - Contract hiện tại đang dùng một danh mục: `products.category_id`.

2. Khách vãng lai có được giữ giỏ hàng bằng `session_id` không?
   - Contract hiện tại có hỗ trợ guest cart.

3. Sau đăng ký có tự động đăng nhập không?
   - Contract hiện tại: đăng ký xong trả user, chưa trả token.

4. Xóa sản phẩm là xóa cứng hay ẩn sản phẩm?
   - Đề xuất: nếu sản phẩm đã có trong order thì chỉ soft delete hoặc `is_active = false`.

5. Có cần customer tự hủy đơn không?
   - PRD hiện chưa yêu cầu. Contract hiện chưa có endpoint customer cancel order.

Các điểm cần chốt trước khi frontend/backend bắt đầu code:

- Category: một hay nhiều danh mục cho một sản phẩm.
- Register flow: có auto-login hay không.
- Product delete strategy: hard delete hay soft delete.
- Có cần customer tự hủy đơn hay không.
- Có cần endpoint riêng cho homepage sections hay frontend tự gọi `/products?is_featured=true` và `/products?sort=newest`.

# Technical Architecture & API Contract — e-commerce-web MVP

## 1. Document Metadata

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Project | `e-commerce-web` |
| Phase | Wave 1 Planning |
| Owner | Technical Architecture & API Contract Agent |
| Input | `docs/prd.md`, `docs/acceptance-criteria.md` |
| Output | Architecture spec + API Contract |
| Status | Locked for Wave 1 MVP |

---

## 2. Architecture Overview

`e-commerce-web` là MVP website bán hàng chạy local trên Windows Native, gồm:

- Customer frontend
- Admin frontend
- Backend REST API
- SQLite database
- JWT authentication
- Role-based authorization

Backend cung cấp API cho frontend customer/admin. Giỏ hàng MVP được xử lý phía frontend bằng local state hoặc `localStorage`; backend chỉ nhận dữ liệu giỏ hàng khi checkout và tự validate/tính toán lại đơn hàng.

---

## 3. Recommended Tech Stack

> Stack có thể được điều chỉnh bởi Backend/Frontend Agent nếu PM Orchestrator phê duyệt, nhưng API Contract trong tài liệu này không được tự ý thay đổi.

### 3.1 Frontend

| Layer | Recommendation |
|---|---|
| Framework | React |
| Build Tool | Vite |
| Language | TypeScript preferred |
| Routing | React Router |
| State | Local state / Context / lightweight store |
| Styling | CSS / Tailwind optional |
| HTTP Client | `fetch` or Axios |

### 3.2 Backend

| Layer | Recommendation |
|---|---|
| Runtime | Node.js |
| Framework | Express.js |
| Language | TypeScript preferred |
| Database | SQLite |
| ORM | Prisma or equivalent |
| Auth | JWT |
| Password Hashing | bcrypt |
| Validation | Zod / Joi / express-validator |

### 3.3 Database

| Requirement | Value |
|---|---|
| DB Engine | SQLite |
| Local file | `dev.db` or equivalent |
| Migration | Required |
| Seed | Required |

---

## 5. Core Business Decisions

### 5.1 Authentication

- Backend issues JWT after login.
- JWT contains minimum claims:
  - `sub`: user ID
  - `email`
  - `role`
- Frontend sends token using:

```http
Authorization: Bearer <token>
```

### 5.2 Roles

Supported roles:

```txt
CUSTOMER
ADMIN
```

### 5.3 Cart

- No backend cart persistence in MVP.
- Cart is stored in frontend local state/localStorage.
- Checkout endpoint receives cart items as product IDs and quantities.
- Backend recalculates prices and total.

### 5.4 Payment

Only COD is supported.

```txt
COD
```

### 5.5 Order Status

```txt
PENDING
CONFIRMED
SHIPPING
COMPLETED
CANCELLED
```

New orders default to `PENDING`.

### 5.6 Soft Delete / Active Flag

For MVP:

- Products should use `isActive`.
- Categories should use `isActive`.
- Customer-facing APIs only return active categories/products.
- Admin APIs may return active and inactive records.

---

## 6. Data Model

## 6.1 Entity: User

```ts
type UserRole = "CUSTOMER" | "ADMIN";

interface User {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  email: string;
  passwordHash: string;
  role: UserRole;
  createdAt: string;
  updatedAt: string;
}
```

### Constraints

- `email` unique.
- `passwordHash` never returned in API responses.
- `role` must be `CUSTOMER` or `ADMIN`.

---

## 6.2 Entity: Category

```ts
interface Category {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  description?: string | null;
  isActive: boolean;
  createdAt: string;
  updatedAt: string;
}
```

### Constraints

- `name` required.
- `name` should be unique or at least non-empty.
- Customer APIs return only `isActive = true`.

---

## 6.3 Entity: Product

```ts
interface Product {
  id: string;
  categoryId: string;
  name: string;
  description: string;
  price: number;
  stock: number;
  imageUrl: string;
  isActive: boolean;
  createdAt: string;
  updatedAt: string;
}
```

### Constraints

- `name` required.
- `categoryId` must reference existing category.
- `price >= 0`.
- `stock >= 0`.
- Customer APIs return only `isActive = true`.

---

## 6.4 Entity: Order

```ts
type OrderStatus =
  | "PENDING"
  | "CONFIRMED"
  | "SHIPPING"
  | "COMPLETED"
  | "CANCELLED";

type PaymentMethod = "COD";

interface Order {
  id: string;
  userId: string;
  status: OrderStatus;
  paymentMethod: PaymentMethod;
  totalAmount: number;
  receiverName: string;
  receiverPhone: string;
  shippingAddress: string;
  note?: string | null;
  createdAt: string;
  updatedAt: string;
}
```

### Constraints

- `userId` references `User`.
- Default `status = PENDING`.
- `paymentMethod = COD` for MVP.
- `totalAmount` is calculated by backend.

---

## 6.5 Entity: OrderItem

```ts
interface OrderItem {
  id: string;
  orderId: string;
  productId: string;
  productName: string;
  productImageUrl: string;
  unitPrice: number;
  quantity: number;
  lineTotal: number;
  createdAt: string;
}
```

### Constraints

- `quantity > 0`.
- `unitPrice` is snapshot price at checkout time.
- `lineTotal = unitPrice * quantity`.
- Order items are immutable after order creation.

---

## 7. API General Contract

## 7.1 Base URL

```txt
http://localhost:<BACKEND_PORT>/api
```

Suggested backend port:

```txt
4000
```

---

## 7.2 Content Type

All request and response bodies use JSON.

```http
Content-Type: application/json
```

---

## 7.3 Authentication Header

```http
Authorization: Bearer <jwt>
```

---

## 7.4 Standard Success Response

APIs may return direct resources or this wrapper. To keep frontend consistent, use the wrapper below.

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": {}
}
```

For list endpoints:

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": [],
  "meta": {
    "page": 1,
    "limit": 20,
    "total": 100,
    "totalPages": 5
  }
}
```

---

## 7.5 Standard Error Response

```json
{
  "success": false,
  "error": {
    "code": "VALIDATION_ERROR",
    "message": "Invalid request payload",
    "details": [
      {
        "field": "email",
        "message": "Email is required"
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

### Common Error Codes

| Code | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `VALIDATION_ERROR` | Input validation failed |
| `UNAUTHORIZED` | Missing/invalid/expired token |
| `FORBIDDEN` | Authenticated but insufficient role |
| `NOT_FOUND` | Resource not found |
| `CONFLICT` | Resource conflict, e.g. duplicate email |
| `OUT_OF_STOCK` | Product stock insufficient |
| `BAD_REQUEST` | Invalid business request |
| `INTERNAL_ERROR` | Unexpected server error |

---

## 7.6 HTTP Status Codes

| Status | Usage |
|---|---|
| `200 OK` | Successful read/update/delete |
| `201 Created` | Resource created |
| `400 Bad Request` | Invalid business request |
| `401 Unauthorized` | Missing/invalid token |
| `403 Forbidden` | Role not allowed |
| `404 Not Found` | Resource not found |
| `409 Conflict` | Duplicate/conflicting data |
| `422 Unprocessable Entity` | Validation error |
| `500 Internal Server Error` | Server error |

# 8.4 Customer Order APIs

## 8.4.1 Checkout COD

```http
POST /api/orders
```

### Auth

Customer.

### Request

```json
{
  "paymentMethod": "COD",
  "receiverName": "Nguyen Van A",
  "receiverPhone": "0909123456",
  "shippingAddress": "123 Nguyen Trai, District 1, HCMC",
  "note": "Call before delivery",
  "items": [
    {
      "productId": "prd_001",
      "quantity": 2
    }
  ]
}
```

### Validation

| Field | Rule |
|---|---|
| `paymentMethod` | required, must be `COD` |
| `receiverName` | required |
| `receiverPhone` | required |
| `shippingAddress` | required |
| `items` | required, non-empty array |
| `items[].productId` | required |
| `items[].quantity` | required, integer, `> 0` |

### Backend Rules

- Must use authenticated customer as `userId`.
- Must verify product exists.
- Product must be active.
- Quantity must not exceed current stock.
- Backend calculates `unitPrice`, `lineTotal`, and `totalAmount`.
- Backend decrements stock after successful order creation.
- New order status is `PENDING`.

### Success Response — `201 Created`

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "order": {
      "id": "ord_001",
      "userId": "usr_001",
      "status": "PENDING",
      "paymentMethod": "COD",
      "totalAmount": 1000000,
      "receiverName": "Nguyen Van A",
      "receiverPhone": "0909123456",
      "shippingAddress": "123 Nguyen Trai, District 1, HCMC",
      "note": "Call before delivery",
      "createdAt": "2026-05-21T10:00:00.000Z",
      "updatedAt": "2026-05-21T10:00:00.000Z",
      "items": [
        {
          "id": "oit_001",
          "orderId": "ord_001",
          "productId": "prd_001",
          "productName": "Running Shoes",
          "productImageUrl": "https://example.com/shoes.jpg",
          "unitPrice": 500000,
          "quantity": 2,
          "lineTotal": 1000000
        }
      ]
    }
  }
}
```

### Error Responses

| Status | Code |
|---|---|
| `401` | `UNAUTHORIZED` |
| `403` | `FORBIDDEN` |
| `400` | `OUT_OF_STOCK` |
| `404` | `NOT_FOUND` |
| `422` | `VALIDATION_ERROR` |

# 9. Authorization Matrix

| Endpoint Group | Guest | Customer | Admin |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| `POST /auth/register` | yes | yes | yes |
| `POST /auth/login` | yes | yes | yes |
| `GET /categories` | yes | yes | yes |
| `GET /products` | yes | yes | yes |
| `GET /products/{id}` | yes | yes | yes |
| `POST /orders` | no | yes | no |
| `GET /orders/my` | no | yes | no |
| `GET /orders/my/{id}` | no | yes | no |
| `/admin/dashboard` | no | no | yes |
| `/admin/categories/*` | no | no | yes |
| `/admin/products/*` | no | no | yes |
| `/admin/orders/*` | no | no | yes |
| `/admin/customers/*` | no | no | yes |

---

# 10. Database Schema Draft

The backend agent may implement using Prisma or raw SQL, but the schema must preserve these relationships.

```txt
users
- id PK
- name
- email UNIQUE
- password_hash
- role
- created_at
- updated_at

categories
- id PK
- name
- description nullable
- is_active
- created_at
- updated_at

products
- id PK
- category_id FK -> categories.id
- name
- description
- price
- stock
- image_url
- is_active
- created_at
- updated_at

orders
- id PK
- user_id FK -> users.id
- status
- payment_method
- total_amount
- receiver_name
- receiver_phone
- shipping_address
- note nullable
- created_at
- updated_at

order_items
- id PK
- order_id FK -> orders.id
- product_id FK -> products.id
- product_name
- product_image_url
- unit_price
- quantity
- line_total
- created_at
```

---

# 11. Seed Data Requirements

Seed script must create at least:

```txt
1 admin user
1 customer user
2+ categories
4+ active products
```

Suggested seed accounts:

```txt
Admin:
email: admin@example.com
password: Admin123
role: ADMIN

Customer:
email: customer@example.com
password: Customer123
role: CUSTOMER
```

Passwords must be hashed.

---

# 12. Frontend Route Recommendation

## 12.1 Customer Site

```txt
/
 /products
 /products/:id
 /cart
 /login
 /register
 /checkout
 /orders
 /orders/:id
```

## 12.2 Admin Site

```txt
/admin/login
/admin
/admin/dashboard
/admin/categories
/admin/products
/admin/products/new
/admin/products/:id/edit
/admin/orders
/admin/orders/:id
/admin/customers
```

---

# 13. Validation Rules Summary

## 13.1 Auth

| Field | Rule |
|---|---|
| `name` | required for register |
| `email` | valid email |
| `password` | min 6 chars |

## 13.2 Category

| Field | Rule |
|---|---|
| `name` | required |
| `isActive` | boolean |

## 13.3 Product

| Field | Rule |
|---|---|
| `categoryId` | required, valid |
| `name` | required |
| `description` | required |
| `price` | number >= 0 |
| `stock` | integer >= 0 |
| `imageUrl` | required |
| `isActive` | boolean |

## 13.4 Checkout

| Field | Rule |
|---|---|
| `receiverName` | required |
| `receiverPhone` | required |
| `shippingAddress` | required |
| `paymentMethod` | `COD` only |
| `items` | non-empty array |
| `items[].quantity` | integer > 0 |

---

# 14. Security Requirements

- Never return `passwordHash` or `password_hash`.
- Hash passwords before storing.
- JWT secret must come from environment variable.
- Admin APIs must verify role `ADMIN`.
- Customer order APIs must verify owner.
- Backend must not trust frontend total price.
- Backend must handle validation errors consistently.
- Server errors must not leak stack traces in API response.

---

# 15. Environment Variables

Suggested backend `.env`:

```env
PORT=4000
DATABASE_URL="file:./dev.db"
JWT_SECRET="replace-with-local-secret"
JWT_EXPIRES_IN="7d"
```

---

# 16. Locked API Contract Notes

The following decisions are locked for Wave 1:

1. Auth uses JWT bearer token.
2. Roles are `CUSTOMER` and `ADMIN`.
3. Cart is frontend/local only.
4. Checkout endpoint is `POST /api/orders`.
5. Customer order endpoints are under `/api/orders/my`.
6. Admin endpoints are under `/api/admin`.
7. Product/category delete behavior should be soft delete/deactivate where practical.
8. Order statuses are:
   - `PENDING`
   - `CONFIRMED`
   - `SHIPPING`
   - `COMPLETED`
   - `CANCELLED`
9. Payment method is `COD` only.
10. Standard error response must follow the contract in section 7.5.

Any change to this API Contract requires PM Orchestrator approval before Frontend/Backend implementation changes.

---

# `projects/active/e-commerce-web/docs/technical-spec.md`

```md
# Technical Specification — E-Commerce Web MVP

## 1. Document Control

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Project | e-commerce-web |
| Phase | W1-ARCH-001 |
| Status | Draft for implementation |
| Owner | Architecture Agent |
| Last Updated | 2026-05-21 |

---

## 2. System Overview

The MVP is a web-based e-commerce application that allows users to:

- Browse products.
- View product details.
- Add products to cart.
- Manage cart quantities.
- Checkout using customer/shipping information.
- Create orders.
- View order confirmation.

The system is designed as a decoupled frontend/backend architecture.

---

## 3. Architecture Goals

### Functional Goals

- Support product catalog browsing.
- Support cart management.
- Support checkout and order placement.
- Provide clear REST API contract between frontend and backend.

### Non-Functional Goals

- Simple MVP architecture.
- Stateless backend API.
- Relational database for transactional consistency.
- API responses must be predictable and typed.
- Clear error handling.
- Ready for future extensions: authentication, payment gateway, admin panel, inventory management.

---

## 4. High-Level Architecture

```txt
+-------------------+
|    Web Browser    |
|  React Frontend   |
+---------+---------+
          |
          | HTTPS / REST JSON
          |
+---------v---------+
|   Backend API     |
| Node.js / Express |
+---------+---------+
          |
          | SQL
          |
+---------v---------+
|   PostgreSQL DB   |
+-------------------+
```

---

## 5. Recommended Technology Stack

## 5.1 Frontend

| Area | Technology |
|---|---|
| Framework | React |
| Language | TypeScript |
| Routing | React Router |
| State Management | React Context or Zustand |
| API Client | Fetch or Axios |
| Styling | CSS Modules / Tailwind CSS |
| Build Tool | Vite |

## 5.2 Backend

| Area | Technology |
|---|---|
| Runtime | Node.js |
| Framework | Express.js |
| Language | TypeScript |
| Validation | Zod / Joi |
| ORM | Prisma |
| Database | PostgreSQL |
| Testing | Jest / Supertest |

## 5.3 Infrastructure

| Area | Technology |
|---|---|
| Local Dev | Docker Compose |
| Deployment | Render / Railway / Fly.io / Vercel split deployment |
| API Format | REST JSON |
| Auth | Not included in MVP |

---

## 6. Application Modules

## 6.1 Frontend Modules

```txt
src/
  api/
    client.ts
    products.api.ts
    cart.api.ts
    orders.api.ts
  components/
    Header.tsx
    ProductCard.tsx
    CartItem.tsx
    QuantitySelector.tsx
    ErrorMessage.tsx
    LoadingState.tsx
  pages/
    HomePage.tsx
    ProductListPage.tsx
    ProductDetailPage.tsx
    CartPage.tsx
    CheckoutPage.tsx
    OrderConfirmationPage.tsx
    NotFoundPage.tsx
  store/
    cartStore.ts
  types/
    product.ts
    cart.ts
    order.ts
  utils/
    currency.ts
```

### Frontend Responsibilities

- Render product list and product detail pages.
- Maintain cart state.
- Call backend APIs.
- Validate checkout form on client side.
- Display loading, empty, and error states.
- Redirect to order confirmation after successful checkout.

---

## 6.2 Backend Modules

```txt
src/
  app.ts
  server.ts
  config/
    env.ts
    database.ts
  routes/
    products.routes.ts
    cart.routes.ts
    orders.routes.ts
  controllers/
    products.controller.ts
    cart.controller.ts
    orders.controller.ts
  services/
    products.service.ts
    cart.service.ts
    orders.service.ts
  repositories/
    products.repository.ts
    cart.repository.ts
    orders.repository.ts
  validators/
    cart.validator.ts
    order.validator.ts
  middleware/
    error.middleware.ts
    requestId.middleware.ts
    notFound.middleware.ts
  types/
    api.ts
```

### Backend Responsibilities

- Expose REST APIs.
- Validate incoming requests.
- Fetch and persist data.
- Calculate cart/order totals server-side.
- Return consistent error responses.
- Prevent checkout with invalid products or unavailable stock.

---

## 7. Data Flow

## 7.1 Product Browsing

```txt
Frontend Product List Page
  -> GET /api/products
  -> Backend Product Service
  -> Database products
  -> JSON response
  -> Render product cards
```

## 7.2 Product Detail

```txt
Frontend Product Detail Page
  -> GET /api/products/:productId
  -> Backend Product Service
  -> Database products
  -> JSON response
  -> Render product detail
```

## 7.3 Cart Management

For MVP, cart is persisted in backend using an anonymous `cartId`.

```txt
Frontend
  -> POST /api/carts
  -> receives cartId

Frontend
  -> POST /api/carts/:cartId/items
  -> backend validates product
  -> upserts cart item
  -> returns updated cart
```

Frontend stores `cartId` in localStorage.

---

## 7.4 Checkout

```txt
Frontend Checkout Page
  -> POST /api/orders
  -> Backend validates cart + customer info
  -> Creates order and order_items
  -> Clears cart
  -> Returns order confirmation
```

---

## 8. API Conventions

Base URL:

```txt
/api
```

Content Type:

```txt
application/json
```

All success responses use:

```json
{
  "data": {},
  "meta": {}
}
```

All error responses use:

```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "VALIDATION_ERROR",
    "message": "Human-readable message",
    "details": []
  }
}
```

---

## 9. HTTP Status Code Guidelines

| Code | Usage |
|---|---|
| 200 | Successful read/update/delete |
| 201 | Resource created |
| 400 | Invalid request |
| 404 | Resource not found |
| 409 | Business rule conflict |
| 422 | Validation error |
| 500 | Server error |

---

## 10. Business Rules

## 10.1 Product

- Product must have a name, price, SKU, and active status.
- Only active products are returned to customers.
- Price must be non-negative.
- Stock quantity must be non-negative.

## 10.2 Cart

- Anonymous cart is created before adding items.
- Cart item quantity must be at least 1.
- Adding an existing product to cart increases or replaces quantity depending on endpoint behavior.
- Cart total is calculated server-side.
- Cart cannot contain inactive products.

## 10.3 Checkout

- Checkout requires:
  - Customer name.
  - Customer email.
  - Shipping address.
  - Non-empty cart.
- Order totals are calculated server-side.
- Order captures product name, SKU, and unit price at purchase time.
- Cart is cleared after successful order creation.

---

## 11. Security Considerations

MVP does not include authentication. However:

- Validate and sanitize all input.
- Never trust frontend-provided prices.
- Use server-calculated totals.
- Use parameterized SQL or ORM.
- Enable CORS only for approved frontend origin.
- Do not expose stack traces in production.
- Use HTTPS in deployed environments.

---

## 12. Performance Considerations

- Product list supports pagination.
- Product query should index:
  - `slug`
  - `is_active`
  - `created_at`
- Cart and order queries should index:
  - `cart_id`
  - `order_number`
  - `created_at`

---

## 13. Observability

Minimum MVP logging:

- Request method/path/status/duration.
- Request ID.
- Error logs with stack trace internally.
- Order creation event.

Recommended response header:

```txt
X-Request-Id
```

---

## 14. Environment Variables

```txt
NODE_ENV=development
PORT=3000
DATABASE_URL=postgresql://user:password@localhost:5432/ecommerce
CORS_ORIGIN=http://localhost:5173
```

---

## 15. MVP Exclusions

The following are intentionally out of scope for MVP:

- User login/register.
- Payment gateway integration.
- Coupons/discounts.
- Product reviews.
- Admin panel.
- Shipment tracking.
- Multi-currency.
- Tax calculation.
- Email notifications.

---

## 16. Implementation Sequence

1. Database schema and migrations.
2. Backend product APIs.
3. Backend cart APIs.
4. Backend order/checkout APIs.
5. Frontend product list/detail.
6. Frontend cart flow.
7. Frontend checkout flow.
8. Integration testing.
9. Acceptance criteria validation.

---

## 17. Architecture Quality Gate

```txt
W1-ARCH-001: READY_FOR_IMPLEMENTATION
Technical Spec: COMPLETED
API Contract: COMPLETED
Database Schema: COMPLETED
Task Board: COMPLETED
```
```

# `projects/active/e-commerce-web/docs/api-contract.md`

```md
# API Contract — E-Commerce Web MVP

## 1. Overview

This document defines the REST API contract between frontend and backend for the E-Commerce Web MVP.

Base URL:

```txt
/api
```

Content Type:

```txt
application/json
```

API Version:

```txt
v1 implicit
```

---

## 2. Response Envelope

## 2.1 Success Response

```json
{
  "data": {},
  "meta": {}
}
```

For collection endpoints:

```json
{
  "data": [],
  "meta": {
    "page": 1,
    "limit": 20,
    "total": 100,
    "totalPages": 5
  }
}
```

## 2.2 Error Response

```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "VALIDATION_ERROR",
    "message": "Validation failed",
    "details": [
      {
        "field": "email",
        "message": "Invalid email format"
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

---

## 3. Error Codes

| Code | Meaning |
|---|---|
| VALIDATION_ERROR | Request validation failed |
| PRODUCT_NOT_FOUND | Product does not exist or is inactive |
| CART_NOT_FOUND | Cart does not exist |
| CART_EMPTY | Cart has no items |
| INSUFFICIENT_STOCK | Requested quantity exceeds stock |
| ORDER_NOT_FOUND | Order does not exist |
| INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR | Unexpected server error |

---

## 4. Data Types

## 4.1 Money

All money values are returned as decimal numbers with two decimal precision.

Example:

```json
19.99
```

Recommended database representation: integer cents or `DECIMAL(10,2)`.

---

## 4.2 Product Object

```json
{
  "id": "prd_123",
  "sku": "TSHIRT-BASIC-BLK",
  "name": "Basic Black T-Shirt",
  "slug": "basic-black-t-shirt",
  "description": "Comfortable cotton t-shirt.",
  "price": 19.99,
  "imageUrl": "https://example.com/images/basic-black-tshirt.jpg",
  "stockQuantity": 25,
  "isActive": true,
  "createdAt": "2026-05-21T10:00:00.000Z",
  "updatedAt": "2026-05-21T10:00:00.000Z"
}
```

---

## 4.3 Cart Object

```json
{
  "id": "cart_123",
  "items": [
    {
      "id": "citem_123",
      "productId": "prd_123",
      "sku": "TSHIRT-BASIC-BLK",
      "name": "Basic Black T-Shirt",
      "imageUrl": "https://example.com/images/basic-black-tshirt.jpg",
      "unitPrice": 19.99,
      "quantity": 2,
      "lineTotal": 39.98
    }
  ],
  "subtotal": 39.98,
  "totalItems": 2,
  "createdAt": "2026-05-21T10:00:00.000Z",
  "updatedAt": "2026-05-21T10:05:00.000Z"
}
```

## 4.4 Order Object

```json
{

      "name": "Basic Black T-Shirt",
      "unitPrice": 19.99,
      "quantity": 2,

## 6.3 Add Item to Cart

```txt

```

| Name | Type | Required |

```json

  "quantity": 1
}
```

### Validation Rules

| Field | Rule |
|---|---|
| productId | Required string |
| quantity | Required integer, min 1 |

### Behavior

- If product is not already in cart, create cart item.
- If product already exists in cart, increase existing quantity by requested quantity.
- Backend validates stock availability.

# `projects/active/e-commerce-web/docs/database-schema.md`

```md
# Database Schema — E-Commerce Web MVP

## 1. Overview

Database: PostgreSQL

The database stores:

- Product catalog.
- Anonymous shopping carts.
- Cart items.
- Customer orders.
- Order items.

---

## 2. Entity Relationship Diagram

```txt
products
  1 ────< cart_items
  1 ────< order_items

carts
  1 ────< cart_items

orders
  1 ────< order_items
```

---

## 3. Tables

## 3.1 products

Stores product catalog data.

```sql
CREATE TABLE products (
  id VARCHAR(40) PRIMARY KEY,
  sku VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
  name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
  slug VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
  description TEXT,
  price DECIMAL(10, 2) NOT NULL CHECK (price >= 0),
  image_url TEXT,
  stock_quantity INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 CHECK (stock_quantity >= 0),
  is_active BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT TRUE,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);
```

### Indexes

```sql
CREATE INDEX idx_products_is_active ON products(is_active);
CREATE INDEX idx_products_created_at ON products(created_at);
CREATE INDEX idx_products_name ON products(name);
CREATE INDEX idx_products_slug ON products(slug);
```

---

## 3.2 carts

Stores anonymous carts.

```sql
CREATE TABLE carts (
  id VARCHAR(40) PRIMARY KEY,
  status VARCHAR(30) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'ACTIVE',
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
  expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ
);
```

### Constraints

```sql
ALTER TABLE carts
ADD CONSTRAINT chk_carts_status
CHECK (status IN ('ACTIVE', 'ORDERED', 'ABANDONED'));
```

### Indexes

```sql
CREATE INDEX idx_carts_status ON carts(status);
CREATE INDEX idx_carts_created_at ON carts(created_at);
```

---

## 3.3 cart_items

Stores items in a cart.

```sql
CREATE TABLE cart_items (
  id VARCHAR(40) PRIMARY KEY,
  cart_id VARCHAR(40) NOT NULL REFERENCES carts(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  product_id VARCHAR(40) NOT NULL REFERENCES products(id),
  quantity INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK (quantity > 0),
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),

  CONSTRAINT uq_cart_items_cart_product UNIQUE (cart_id, product_id)
);
```

### Indexes

```sql
CREATE INDEX idx_cart_items_cart_id ON cart_items(cart_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_cart_items_product_id ON cart_items(product_id);
```

---

## 3.4 orders

Stores customer order header.

```sql
CREATE TABLE orders (
  id VARCHAR(40) PRIMARY KEY,
  order_number VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL UNIQUE,

  customer_name VARCHAR(120) NOT NULL,
  customer_email VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
  customer_phone VARCHAR(30),

  shipping_line1 VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
  shipping_line2 VARCHAR(255),
  shipping_city VARCHAR(120) NOT NULL,
  shipping_state VARCHAR(120),
  shipping_postal_code VARCHAR(30),
  shipping_country VARCHAR(3) NOT NULL,

  subtotal DECIMAL(10, 2) NOT NULL CHECK (subtotal >= 0),
  status VARCHAR(30) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'PENDING',

  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);
```

### Constraints

```sql
ALTER TABLE orders
ADD CONSTRAINT chk_orders_status
CHECK (status IN ('PENDING', 'CONFIRMED', 'CANCELLED'));
```

### Indexes

```sql
CREATE INDEX idx_orders_order_number ON orders(order_number);
CREATE INDEX idx_orders_customer_email ON orders(customer_email);
CREATE INDEX idx_orders_status ON orders(status);
CREATE INDEX idx_orders_created_at ON orders(created_at);
```

---

## 3.5 order_items

Stores immutable order line items.

```sql
CREATE TABLE order_items (
  id VARCHAR(40) PRIMARY KEY,
  order_id VARCHAR(40) NOT NULL REFERENCES orders(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  product_id VARCHAR(40) REFERENCES products(id),

  sku VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
  name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
  unit_price DECIMAL(10, 2) NOT NULL CHECK (unit_price >= 0),
  quantity INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK (quantity > 0),
  line_total DECIMAL(10, 2) NOT NULL CHECK (line_total >= 0),

  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);
```

### Indexes

```sql
CREATE INDEX idx_order_items_order_id ON order_items(order_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_order_items_product_id ON order_items(product_id);
```

---

## 4. ID Format

Application-generated IDs are recommended.

| Entity | Prefix | Example |
|---|---|---|
| Product | prd_ | prd_01HXABC123 |
| Cart | cart_ | cart_01HXABC123 |
| Cart Item | citem_ | citem_01HXABC123 |
| Order | ord_ | ord_01HXABC123 |
| Order Item | oitem_ | oitem_01HXABC123 |

Recommended generator: ULID or UUID with prefix.

---

## 5. Order Number Format

Order number format:

```txt
EC-YYYYMMDD-XXXXXX
```

Example:

```txt
EC-20260521-000001
```

Order number must be unique.

---

## 6. Derived Fields

The following are calculated in backend service layer and should not be trusted from frontend:

| Field | Source |
|---|---|
| cart subtotal | sum(product.price * cart_item.quantity) |
| cart totalItems | sum(cart_item.quantity) |
| order subtotal | sum(order_item.line_total) |
| order item line_total | unit_price * quantity |

---

## 7. Checkout Transaction

Order creation must run in a database transaction:

```txt
BEGIN
  1. Fetch cart with items.
  2. Validate cart exists and is ACTIVE.
  3. Validate cart is not empty.
  4. Validate each product is active.
  5. Validate stock quantity.
  6. Create order.
  7. Create order_items using product snapshot values.
  8. Decrement product stock quantity.
  9. Mark cart as ORDERED.
  10. Delete or retain cart_items depending on implementation preference.
COMMIT
```

Recommended MVP behavior:

- Keep `cart_items` for audit until cart deletion is needed.
- Mark cart `status = 'ORDERED'`.
- Prevent further modifications to non-ACTIVE carts.

---

## 8. Seed Data

Example seed products:

```sql
INSERT INTO products (
  id,
  sku,
  name,
  slug,
  description,
  price,
  image_url,
  stock_quantity,
  is_active
) VALUES
(
  'prd_001',
  'TSHIRT-BASIC-BLK',
  'Basic Black T-Shirt',
  'basic-black-t-shirt',
  'Comfortable cotton t-shirt.',
  19.99,
  'https://example.com/images/basic-black-tshirt.jpg',
  25,
  TRUE
),
(
  'prd_002',
  'MUG-WHITE-001',
  'Classic White Mug',
  'classic-white-mug',
  'Durable ceramic mug.',
  9.99,
  'https://example.com/images/classic-white-mug.jpg',
  50,
  TRUE
),
(
  'prd_003',
  'CAP-NAVY-001',
  'Navy Baseball Cap',
  'navy-baseball-cap',
  'Adjustable cotton baseball cap.',
  14.99,
  'https://example.com/images/navy-baseball-cap.jpg',
  30,
  TRUE
);
```

---

## 9. Prisma Model Draft

```prisma
model Product {
  id            String      @id
  sku           String      @unique
  name          String
  slug          String      @unique
  description   String?
  price         Decimal     @db.Decimal(10, 2)
  imageUrl      String?     @map("image_url")
  stockQuantity Int         @default(0) @map("stock_quantity")
  isActive      Boolean     @default(true) @map("is_active")
  createdAt     DateTime    @default(now()) @map("created_at")
  updatedAt     DateTime    @updatedAt @map("updated_at")

  cartItems     CartItem[]
  orderItems    OrderItem[]

  @@map("products")
}

model Cart {
  id        String     @id
  status    String     @default("ACTIVE")
  createdAt DateTime   @default(now()) @map("created_at")
  updatedAt DateTime   @updatedAt @map("updated_at")
  expiresAt DateTime?  @map("expires_at")

  items     CartItem[]

  @@map("carts")
}

model CartItem {
  id        String   @id
  cartId    String   @map("cart_id")
  productId String   @map("product_id")
  quantity  Int
  createdAt DateTime @default(now()) @map("created_at")
  updatedAt DateTime @updatedAt @map("updated_at")

  cart      Cart     @relation(fields: [cartId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  product   Product  @relation(fields: [productId], references: [id])

  @@unique([cartId, productId])
  @@map("cart_items")
}

model Order {
  id                 String      @id
  orderNumber        String      @unique @map("order_number")

  customerName       String      @map("customer_name")
  customerEmail      String      @map("customer_email")
  customerPhone      String?     @map("customer_phone")

  shippingLine1      String      @map("shipping_line1")
  shippingLine2      String?     @map("shipping_line2")
  shippingCity       String      @map("shipping_city")
  shippingState      String?     @map("shipping_state")
  shippingPostalCode String?     @map("shipping_postal_code")
  shippingCountry    String      @map("shipping_country")

  subtotal           Decimal     @db.Decimal(10, 2)
  status             String      @default("PENDING")

  createdAt          DateTime    @default(now()) @map("created_at")
  updatedAt          DateTime    @updatedAt @map("updated_at")

  items              OrderItem[]

  @@map("orders")
}

model OrderItem {
  id        String   @id
  orderId   String   @map("order_id")
  productId String?  @map("product_id")

  sku       String
  name      String
  unitPrice Decimal  @map("unit_price") @db.Decimal(10, 2)
  quantity  Int
  lineTotal Decimal  @map("line_total") @db.Decimal(10, 2)

  createdAt DateTime @default(now()) @map("created_at")

  order     Order    @relation(fields: [orderId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  product   Product? @relation(fields: [productId], references: [id])

  @@map("order_items")
}
```

---

## 10. Migration Notes

- `updated_at` should be updated automatically by ORM or database trigger.
- For high-precision commerce systems, use integer cents. For MVP, `DECIMAL(10,2)` is acceptable.
- Stock decrement during checkout must happen transactionally.
```

frontend-agent.text:
Mình sẽ kiểm tra cấu trúc dự án frontend hiện có, xác định stack và owned paths trước khi triển khai UI theo PRD/API contract.Mình đã nhận PRD và API Contract cho **Website Bán Hàng MVP**.

Tuy nhiên hiện tại mình chưa có quyền/khả năng truy cập filesystem hoặc terminal của project trong phiên làm việc này, nên chưa thể trực tiếp:

- Kiểm tra cấu trúc thư mục frontend.
- Xác định framework đang dùng: React, Next.js, Vite, Vue...
- Tạo/sửa file trong source frontend.
- Chạy `npm install`, `npm run dev`, `npm run build`.
- Cung cấp bằng chứng build/dev server thành công.

Để mình triển khai đúng vai trò **Frontend Agent**, bạn vui lòng cung cấp thêm một trong các thông tin sau:

## 1. Cấu trúc project frontend

Ví dụ:

```txt
frontend/
  package.json
  src/
    ...
```

Hoặc gửi nội dung các file chính:

- `package.json`
- `src/main.tsx` hoặc `src/app/page.tsx`
- `src/App.tsx`
- config router nếu có
- config Tailwind/Vite/Next nếu có

## 2. Stack frontend đang dùng

Bạn xác nhận giúp:

- React + Vite hay Next.js?
- TypeScript có dùng không?
- Tailwind CSS hay CSS module/SCSS?
- Có UI library không? Ant Design, shadcn/ui, MUI?
- State management hiện có không? Zustand/Redux/Context?

## 3. Owned Paths

Theo rule “Chỉ sửa Frontend”, mình cần biết thư mục được phép sửa, ví dụ:

```txt
Owned Paths:
- frontend/**
```

hoặc

```txt
Owned Paths:
- apps/web/**
```

## 4. Backend/API đã có chưa?

Nếu backend chưa chạy, mình có thể triển khai frontend theo API contract kèm:

- API client chuẩn `/api/v1`.
- Mock fallback hoặc mock service tạm thời.
- UI hoàn chỉnh, chờ backend thật.

Nếu backend đã có, cần thêm:

```txt
VITE_API_BASE_URL=http://localhost:xxxx/api/v1
```

hoặc

```txt
NEXT_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL=http://localhost:xxxx/api/v1
```

---

Sau khi có thông tin project, mình sẽ triển khai các phần chính:

## Customer Website

- Trang chủ.
- Danh sách sản phẩm.
- Tìm kiếm/lọc/sắp xếp sản phẩm.
- Chi tiết sản phẩm.
- Giỏ hàng.
- Đăng ký/đăng nhập.
- Checkout COD.
- Đặt hàng thành công.
- Lịch sử đơn hàng.
- Chi tiết đơn hàng.
- Thông tin tài khoản.

## Admin Dashboard

- Login admin.
- Dashboard tổng quan.
- Quản lý danh mục.
- Quản lý sản phẩm.
- Quản lý đơn hàng.
- Quản lý khách hàng.

## Tích hợp API theo contract

Base URL:

```txt
/api/v1
```

Auth:

```http
Authorization: Bearer <access_token>
```

Guest cart:

```http
X-Session-Id: <uuid>
```

## Validation/Form UX

- Validate đăng ký.
- Validate đăng nhập.
- Validate checkout.
- Validate form admin category/product.
- Hiển thị thông báo lỗi/thành công thân thiện.

## Bằng chứng hoàn thành sẽ gồm

Sau khi có quyền chạy lệnh:

```bash
npm install
npm run build
npm run dev
```

Mình sẽ báo cáo kèm:

- Build OK.
- Dev server URL.
- Các route đã kiểm tra.
- Ghi chú lỗi nếu API backend chưa sẵn sàng.

Tôi sẽ kiểm tra cấu trúc project frontend hiện có trước, sau đó chỉ chỉnh trong phần frontend và chạy build để lấy evidence.Mình chưa thể chỉnh sửa trực tiếp trong môi trường của bạn qua API nếu không có quyền truy cập filesystem/tool execution trong phiên này.

Để triển khai đúng vai trò Frontend Agent, mình cần một trong hai cách:

1. **Bạn cung cấp cây thư mục frontend hiện tại** của project:
   ```txt
   D:\Antigravity\LeeJ_MultiAgent\projects\active\e-commerce-web
   ```
   đặc biệt các file như:
   - `package.json`
   - `src/`
   - `vite.config.*`
   - `tsconfig.*`
   - cấu trúc route/component hiện tại

2. Hoặc bật/cung cấp công cụ truy cập file/lệnh để mình có thể:
   - inspect project
   - sửa file trong frontend owned paths
   - chạy:
     ```powershell

     npm run build
     npm run dev
     ```

Sau khi có quyền hoặc thông tin project hiện tại, mình sẽ triển khai frontend theo API Contract đã khóa, bao gồm:

- Customer site:
  - `/`

  - `/cart`
  - `/login`
  - `/register`
  - `/checkout`
  - `/orders`

- Admin site:
  - `/admin/login`
  - `/admin`
  - `/admin/dashboard`
  - `/admin/categories`
  - `/admin/products`
  - `/admin/products/new`
  - `/admin/products/:id/edit`
  - `/admin/orders`
  - `/admin/orders/:id`
  - `/admin/customers`

- Frontend integration:
  - API client dùng base URL `http://localhost:4000/api`
  - JWT token storage
  - role-based route guards
  - localStorage cart
  - checkout COD payload đúng contract
  - admin CRUD/list screens đúng endpoint `/api/admin/*`

- Evidence khi hoàn tất:
  - build OK
  - dev server live nếu chạy được
  - log/command output xác nhận

|---|---|

## 2. Phạm vi Acceptance Criteria cần kiểm thử

QA sẽ kiểm thử các nhóm chính sau khi có môi trường:

### Customer Website

1. Trang chủ.
2. Danh sách sản phẩm.
3. Tìm kiếm sản phẩm.
4. Chi tiết sản phẩm.
5. Giỏ hàng.
6. Đăng ký tài khoản.
7. Đăng nhập/đăng xuất.
8. Thanh toán/đặt hàng COD.
9. Lịch sử đơn hàng.
10. Chi tiết đơn hàng.
11. Responsive desktop/tablet/mobile.

### Admin

1. Đăng nhập admin.
2. Dashboard.
3. Quản lý danh mục.
4. Quản lý sản phẩm.
5. Quản lý đơn hàng.
6. Cập nhật trạng thái đơn hàng.
7. Quản lý khách hàng.
8. Khóa/mở khóa tài khoản.

### Non-functional

1. Bảo mật cơ bản.
2. Validate form.
3. Phân quyền API admin/customer.
4. Không cho xem đơn hàng của người khác.
5. Password phải được hash.
6. Hiệu năng danh sách/tìm kiếm sản phẩm.
7. Xử lý lỗi API thân thiện.
8. Xác nhận trước khi xóa dữ liệu.

## 3. Blockers

### BLOCKER-001 — Chưa có môi trường hoặc source code để kiểm thử

**Mức độ:** Critical  
**Trạng thái:** Open  
**Ảnh hưởng:** Không thể chạy ứng dụng, không thể kiểm thử UI/API, không thể xác nhận Acceptance Criteria.

#### Mô tả

QA chưa có quyền truy cập filesystem, terminal, repository hoặc URL môi trường chạy thực tế. Không thể thực hiện các thao tác:

- Build frontend/backend.
- Chạy dev server.
- Gọi API.
- Kiểm tra database.
- Thực hiện flow mua hàng.
- Đăng nhập admin/customer.
- Ghi nhận console errors/network errors.

#### Thông tin cần cung cấp

Vui lòng cung cấp tối thiểu một trong các phương án sau:

### Phương án A — Cung cấp URL môi trường đang chạy

```txt
Customer site URL:
Admin site URL:
API base URL:
```

Kèm tài khoản test:

```txt
Customer account:
Email:
Password:

Admin account:
Email:
Password:
```

### Phương án B — Cung cấp source code và hướng dẫn chạy local

Cần có:

```txt
Repository/source code
Frontend path
Backend path
Database type
Env file mẫu
Seed data
Run commands
```

Ví dụ:

```bash
cd backend
npm install
npm run migrate
npm run seed
npm run dev

cd frontend
npm install
npm run dev
```

### Phương án C — Cung cấp artifact build/test

Nếu team đã build/test, vui lòng cung cấp:

- Build logs.
- Test logs.
- API collection/Postman.
- Swagger/OpenAPI.
- Screenshot hoặc video demo các flow chính.

## 4. Test Data yêu cầu

Để kiểm thử đầy đủ PRD, QA cần dữ liệu mẫu sau:

### Customer

| Loại tài khoản | Email | Trạng thái |
|---|---|---|
| Customer active | customer@example.com | active |
| Customer locked | locked@example.com | locked |
| Customer khác | customer2@example.com | active |

### Admin

| Loại tài khoản | Email | Role |
|---|---|---|
| Admin | admin@example.com | admin |

### Product

Cần tối thiểu:

| Loại sản phẩm | Điều kiện |
|---|---|
| Sản phẩm còn hàng | stock_quantity > 0 |
| Sản phẩm hết hàng | stock_quantity = 0 |
| Sản phẩm có khuyến mãi | sale_price < original_price |
| Sản phẩm không khuyến mãi | sale_price null hoặc bằng 0 |
| Sản phẩm ẩn | is_active = false |
| Sản phẩm nổi bật | is_featured = true |
| Sản phẩm thuộc nhiều danh mục hoặc ít nhất có category hợp lệ | category_id valid |

### Category

Cần tối thiểu:

| Loại danh mục | Điều kiện |
|---|---|
| Danh mục active | is_active = true |
| Danh mục inactive | is_active = false |
| Danh mục có sản phẩm | dùng để test không cho xóa |
| Danh mục không có sản phẩm | dùng để test xóa thành công |

### Order

Cần hoặc có thể tạo trong quá trình test:

| Trạng thái | Mục đích |
|---|---|
| Chờ xác nhận | Test update sang Đã xác nhận hoặc Đã hủy |
| Đã xác nhận | Test update sang Đang giao hàng hoặc Đã hủy |
| Đang giao hàng | Test update sang Hoàn tất |
| Hoàn tất | Test không cho update tiếp |
| Đã hủy | Test không cho update tiếp |

# 8. Đề nghị bước tiếp theo

Để QA bắt đầu kiểm thử, vui lòng cung cấp:

```txt
1. Repository hoặc source code.
2. Hướng dẫn chạy frontend/backend local trên Windows.
3. File .env.example.
